dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9543 IH: To amend the Act of July 5, 1884, to permit the imposition of taxes on vessels operating as tourist accommodations, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Case introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Act of July 5, 1884, to permit the imposition of taxes on vessels operating as tourist accommodations, and for other purposes.

section.header: Imposition of taxes on vessels operating as tourist accommodations
section.text: Section 4 of the Act of July 5, 1884 (33 U.S.C. 5), is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (2)(C), by striking or at the end;
in paragraph (3), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: taxes on vessels operating as cruise ships, or the passengers or crew of such vessels.
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Any non-Federal interest may levy and collect any of the impositions in paragraphs (b)(1), (b)(2), (b)(3), and (b)(4) under clauses 2 and 3 of section 10, and under clause 3 of section 8, of Article 1 of the Constitution.
In this section, the term cruise ship means any vessel that—

paragraph.text: docks at any port in the United States; and
charges a fee for and provides an accommodation or living quarter on such vessel to transients.
